feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Fiori
feed.subtitle: SAP Fiori Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Business Application Studio-Fiori Tools - Fiori app generation broken for RAP-based OData V4 service
entry.content:
Hi,

Environment details:

Backend - SAP S4HANA 2022 FPS02

SAP Business Application Studio Version: 1.93.1
UI5 Version 1.108.30

I am encountering a strange issue in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) while generating a Fiori List Report for a RAP-based OData V4 service. Despite the service being explicitly defined and recognized as OData V4, BAS/Fiori Tools incorrectly treat it as an OData V2 service and generate faulty configurations.

This issue affects all my previously deployed V4 services, which worked successfully earlier. However, when redeploying these services as duplicate apps for testing, BAS incorrectly interprets them as V2 services. As a result, the generated application is broken and fails to function both during preview in BAS and after deployment.

The app fails to load with the following errors:
"[ODataMetadata] initial loading of metadata failed"

"Invalid metadata document."

It is important to note that the same application and service work perfectly fine when previewed locally through Eclipse, which confirms that both the app and the generated OData V4 service, metadata are correct. 

Is anyone else facing a similar problem? App generation and deployment has worked absolutely fine in the past with the exact same steps.

Best Regards,

Saurabh

entry.title: Not able to add more than one data source in sapui5 app in cloud foundry
entry.content:
Dear Team,

I have already developed and deployed a SAPUI5 freestyle application in SAP Cloud Foundry, using the SAP C4C API as the data source. Now, I need to consume some data from an on-premise S/4HANA system. However, when I add a second data source to the existing UI5 MTA application, I encounter a 404 error.

I have referred to community discussions and found that similar issues remain unresolved.

Please assist us in identifying what we are missing in adding the second data source. Attached is the project files for your reference.

Thanks

entry.title: SAPUI5: Date picker produces error "duplicate id" in mobile browsers (but not in desktop browsers)
entry.content:
Dear experts,

In our app there is an oData function import with an import parameter of type date. When the action is initiated, an input field is rendered with datepicker value help, and it opens the usual calendar and clockface dialog:

This works perfectly in the desktop browsers but on mobile devices via SAP Start there is a strange malfunction:

  • the first time the action popup is opened, date picker works normally, i.e. as in screenshots above
  • but if the user performs following sequence:
    1. picks a date
    2. then cancels the action
    3. then reopens the action again,

        the date picker dialog no longer appears. The datepicker icon is seemingly activated when touched (it briefly flickers in colour when you press), but it is unresponsive.

  • if the action is cancelled again and the page is refreshed, everything is again back to normal.

This happens on both Android and iOS.

Please suggest what could be the problem, or how to analyse further. I am currently trying to remote-debug it on Android and I will post any interesting findings.

Update: in remote debugging this comes up:

adding element with duplicate id 'ActionUtil-BLAHBLAHDIBLAH_MY_SERVICE_Entities-<My_Action_Name>-<My_Parameter_Name>-input-PC' -

Thank you, 

BR Petra

entry.title: CREATE button is not appearing after deployed Fiori Application using RAP ODATA with UI Annotation
entry.content:
Used Fiori Element template List Report Object Page

CREATE button is not appearing after deployed List Report Object Fiori Elements Application using RAP ODATA with UI Annotation

1)When I run the RAP Application locally  I am able to see the CREATE button for the Resource section and Hours Section in the Object Page.

2)When I deploy the application in SAP -on premise system 2023 and clicked on the below tile

3)Now the List Report open and clicked on particular record and  enter into the Object page and not able to see the CREATE button for the Resource and Hours section

Please find the attachments in detail

entry.title: Amount is lying in customer account post reversing the billing doc
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have created the billing document then reversed the same but still the amount is lying in customer account.
Thanks in advance;

Kiran

entry.title: I am not able to set default filter values in smartfilterbar
entry.content:
I have following issues with smartfilterbar

1.not able to set the default filter value, though i written code in view.xml

output

2.Autobinding not working on smarttable though i have set enable autobinding true

entry.title: SAP SAM4U Solution | License Reporting
entry.content:
Dear All,

SAM4U Introduction- 

SAP’s software asset manager tool SAM4U is a tailored solution that allows customers to analyze their license consumption compared to SAP license entitlement. It consolidates usage data from customers’ systems and considers data such as SAP user authorizations, SAP users' active use footprints, and information about developer users.

This can be implemented in any SAP S4HANA foundation platform. 

TCI package can be refereed from KBA# 3292019 - SAM4U Data Extractor 

 

Details Steps: 

Implementation Steps-

  1. Implement TCI note 3298562 implementations.
  2. Implement SAP notes 2706317 and 3292019

Post installation steps-

    • Create SAM4U Variant for ABAP Report /SAM4U/BTL_START_URL
    • Activate sicf service /sap/bc/ui5_ui5/sam4u/dashbrd/
    • Activate and Maintain Services odata service /SAM4U/BTL_CLIENT
    • Execute test Odata service
    • Create RFC Connection for Definition for Daemon Registration
    • Execute the program /SAM4U/BTL_DAEMON_MANAGER for Daemon Registration.
    • Daemon Registration for Auto Start Up

Backend system configuration-

  1. Configure RFC connection of type 3 to your SAP systems. 
  2. Create RFC user with access to execute "/SAM4U/BEX_ACTIVE_USE_ANALYSIS" along with S_TOOLS_EX authorization. 
  3. Obtain recent Authorization rule set by following note 3113382(check administration section of the guide for more detials "User Guide - SAM4U Tool-V2-L"
  4. Start SAM4U Fiori Application

We can analyze consolidated license consumption data from various SAP backend systems and compare it with current licensing information using one of the following methods:

  1. SAP users’ authorizations
  2. SAP active usage of the systems
  3. SAP manual users’ classification
  4. SAP Engines usage
  5. SAP HANA EE usage

entry.title: DM Core Plugin Extension - how to avoid deploying all plugins from SAP github code in mta deployment
entry.content:
Hello DM Plugin experts,

SAP Digital Manufacturing provides plugin code on github, which can be extended. 
But what is the best practice to avoid deploying all plugins (from github samples) together as mta deployment. We don't want to have any side effects on other POD plugins.

https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/pod-plugin-developer-s-guide/implementing-core-plugin-extensions

@vivekananda_panigrahy 

Regards,

Deep Agrawal

entry.title: Onetime Deduction Uploads
entry.content:
Hello all,

Need to ask you all advise on specific usecase

We have a process where by we have to upload onetime deduction by Non-HR user more than one location on regular basis.

Landscape : successfactors with a Backend ECC system, payroll on ECC and is processed on Payroll Control center.

We always had a minimal uploads to do so we sought LSMW however with this new deduction which encompasses multiple Non HR user it means multiple upload files which is not efficient.

I have seen Fiori lets users upload document which use a service to upload the file similarly do we have any SAP delivered service which we can resuse to upload IT 0015 to ECC?

My idea was if the Non HR users upload the file from Fiori , hopefully service will then point/send this data to CPI which then converts data in to IDOC's hoping this Idoc will be sent to ECC.

Let me know if you have any better way.

Many thanks

Ajay

entry.title: BAS - Load error in app using Attachment Services Reuse Component RAP V4 OData Service
entry.content:
Hello Expert,

I have created a Fiori element application based on RAP V4 OData service and my application is working fine and getting data on list page and object page.

We have created the RAP V4 service and created the application based on this. Now I want to add the "Reusable Attachment component" in my object page. And added the configuration same as provided in SAPUI5 demokit for ODATA V4 version. 

https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/sdk/#/topic/d869d7ab3caa48b2a20dc20dfa248380

Now when I am running my application then newly added section getting with blank and in console I am getting error "Attachment reusable" component not found. 

Development Tool: BAS

Updated pom.xml file as well.

Manifest file code:

{
  "_version": "1.65.0",
  "sap.app": {
    "id": "z1tcp",
    "type": "application",
    "i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties",
    "applicationVersion": {
      "version": "${project.version}"
    },
    "title": "{{appTitle}}",
    "description": "{{appDescription}}",
    "resources": "resources.json",
    "sourceTemplate": {
      "id": "@sap/generator-fiori:lrop",
      "version": "1.16.1",
      "toolsId": "87db7502-71fa-4d4a-85c2-f10ca23474b6"
    },
    "dataSources": {
      "mainService": {
        "uri": "/sap/opu/odata4/sap/ui_tradeclaimrequest_v4/srvd/sap/ui_tradeclaimrequest1/0001/",
        "type": "OData",
        "settings": {
          "annotations": [
            "annotation"
          ],
          "localUri": "localService/metadata.xml",
          "odataVersion": "4.0"
        }
      },
      "annotation": {
        "type": "ODataAnnotation",
        "uri": "annotations/annotation.xml",
        "settings": {
          "localUri": "annotations/annotation.xml"
        }
      }
    },
    "ach": "123"
  },
  "sap.ui": {
    "technology": "UI5",
    "icons": {
      "icon": "",
      "favIcon": "",
      "phone": "",
      "phone@2": "",
      "tablet": "",
      "tablet@2": ""
    },
    "deviceTypes": {
      "desktop": true,
      "tablet": true,
      "phone": true
    }
  },
  "sap.ui5": {
    "flexEnabled": true,
    "componentUsages": {
      "attachmentReuseComponent": {
        "name": "sap.se.mi.plm.lib.attachmentservice.attachment.components.stcomponent",
        "settings": {}
      }
    },
    "dependencies": {
      "minUI5Version": "${sap.ui5.dist.version}",
      "libs": {
        "sap.m": {
          "lazy": false
        },
        "sap.ui.core": {
          "lazy": false
        },
        "sap.fe.templates": {
          "lazy": false
        },
        "sap.nw.core.gbt.notes.lib.reuse": {
          "lazy": false
        },
        "sap.se.mi.plm.lib.attachmentservice": {
          "lazy": true
        }
      },
      "components": {
        "sap.se.mi.plm.lib.attachmentservice.attachment.components.stcomponent": {
          "lazy": true
        }
      }
    },
    "contentDensities": {
      "compact": true,
      "cozy": true
    },
    "models": {
      "i18n": {
        "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
        "settings": {
          "bundleName": "z1tcp.i18n.i18n"
        }
      },
      "": {
        "dataSource": "mainService",
        "preload": true,
        "settings": {
          "operationMode": "Server",
          "autoExpandSelect": true,
          "earlyRequests": true
        }
      },
      "@i18n": {
        "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
        "uri": "i18n/i18n.properties"
      }
    },
    "resources": {
      "css": []
    },
    "routing": {
      "config": {},
      "routes": [
        {
          "pattern": ":?query:",
          "name": "C_TradeClaimRequestTPList",
          "target": "C_TradeClaimRequestTPList"
        },
        {
          "pattern": "C_TradeClaimRequestTP({key}):?query:",
          "name": "C_TradeClaimRequestTPObjectPage",
          "target": [
            "C_TradeClaimRequestTPObjectPage",
            "ReuseComponent"
          ]
        }
      ],
      "targets": {
        "C_TradeClaimRequestTPList": {
          "type": "Component",
          "id": "C_TradeClaimRequestTPList",
          "name": "sap.fe.templates.ListReport",
          "options": {
            "settings": {
              "contextPath": "/C_TradeClaimRequestTP",
              "variantManagement": "Page",
              "navigation": {
                "C_TradeClaimRequestTP": {
                  "detail": {
                    "route": "C_TradeClaimRequestTPObjectPage"
                  }
                }
              },
              "controlConfiguration": {
                "@com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.LineItem": {
                  "tableSettings": {
                    "type": "ResponsiveTable",
                    "selectionMode": "Auto",
                    "condensedTableLayout": true
                  }
                }
              }
            }
          }
        },
        "C_TradeClaimRequestTPObjectPage": {
          "type": "Component",
          "id": "C_TradeClaimRequestTPObjectPage",
          "name": "sap.fe.templates.ObjectPage",
          "options": {
            "settings": {
              "editableHeaderContent": false,
              "contextPath": "/C_TradeClaimRequestTP",
              "content": {
                "body": {
                  "sections": {
                    "customSectionReuse": {
                      "title": "{i18n>customSectionReuse}",
                      "embeddedComponent": {
                        "name": "sap.se.mi.plm.lib.attachmentservice.attachment.components.stcomponent",
                        "settings": {
                          "mode": "{= ${ui>/editable}?'C':'D'}",
                          "visible": true
                        }
                      },
                      "position": {
                        "placement": "After",
                        "anchor": "someOtherSection"
                      }
                    },
                    "ReuseComponentContainer": {
                      "position": {
                        "placement": "Before",
                        "anchor": "Attachments"
                      },
                      "title": "Reuse Container",
                      "subSections": {
                        "ReuseContainer": {
                          "id": "ReuseComponentContainer"
                        }
                      }
                    }
                  }
                }
              }
            }
          }
        },
        "ReuseComponent": {
          "type": "Component",
          "id": "Reuse",
          "name": "",
          "options": {
            "settings": {
              "exampleSettings": ""
            }
          },
          "parent": "C_TradeClaimRequestTPObjectPage",
          "controlId": "ReuseComponentContainer",
          "controlAggregation": "blocks",
          "clearControlAggregation": true
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "sap.fiori": {
    "registrationIds": [
      "z1tcp"
    ],
    "archeType": "transactional"
  },
  "sap.fe": {
    "macros": {
      "table": {
        "defaultCreationMode": "InlineCreationRows"
      }
    }
  }
}
 
Error which I am getting: 
Log-dbg.js:497 2024-12-24 20:12:30.218399 The following error occurred while displaying routing target with name 'z1tcp::C_TradeClaimRequestTPObjectPage--fe::CustomSubSection::customSectionReuse::component': ModuleError: failed to load 'sap/se/mi/plm/lib/attachmentservice/attachment/components/stcomponent/Component.js' from ../resources/sap/se/mi/plm/lib/attachmentservice/attachment/components/stcomponent/Component.js: script load error -
 
Could you please help me or share the sample file if I am missing anything.  Thank you.
 
REgards,
Dhiraj

entry.title: Get Response from Asynchronous Outbound WebServiceggest
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am building a Fiori Application which needs to call Asynchronous WS in SAP Backend.

I have a pair of Asynchronous WebService (Inbound and Outbound). I am calling the Asynchronous Inbound WebService from UI5 and it is providing me only Status Code. The relevant Outbound WebService is configured in SAP Backend System and I can find the Response in SRT_MONI. I need the same Response in UI5 to proceed with further logic. 

Please suggest a feasible solution.

 

Thanks,

Ankit.

entry.title: My Inbox Fiori App Performance Issue Due Reading unnecessary Work Items in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We have implemented the My Inbox Fiori application in our S/4HANA system, where multiple workflow tasks  are configured for decisions like leave requests and service entries. However, we are facing a performance issue: the My Inbox app takes an unusually long time to load, even for just four line items.

Our technical consultant investigated the issue and found that the app is reading a significant amount of data from CATSDB work items in the backend. These CATSDB entries contain numerous line items, which seems to be causing the delay.

Since CATSDB-relevant data is not required in our My Inbox application, we are looking for a solution to exclude or filter out this data to improve the app's performance.

Has anyone encountered a similar issue? How can we prevent My Inbox from reading unnecessary CATSDB work items or optimize the data retrieval process? Any advice, configuration tips, or notes would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: File Broken When Download, Upload Excel Files from SAP FIORI
entry.content:
Dear. All

 

Please let me know if you know about the Fiori error.
Downloading an Excel file from FIORI will break it.
There is no problem in Dev environment, but the problem occurs in QA environment.

Among the downloaded files, there is a NaN in the field where the date is not entered.
It's supposed to be blank..

entry.title: SAP FIORI URL Slowness issue while opening in android phone and browsers sometime
entry.content:
We are experiencing inconsistent behavior when accessing the Fiori URL after the recent SSO implementation it was working fine before SSO after its implement its redirect netweaver URL which takes time sometimes and sometimes not.
 
The URL sometimes takes a long time to open, while at other times it opens quickly.
The issue is inconsistent and unpredictable in android devices as well as in the IPHONE devices URL is opening without any delay.
Similar to Android devices, the URL behaves inconsistently, with delays or quick access at random times.
On the organization's Wi-Fi, the URL sometimes does not open at all.
When switching to a cellular 4G connection, the link opens without any issues.
 
Observations:
 
The issue seems specific to Android devices and Windows browsers, as iPhones consistently access the URL without problems and also android phones access URL without any delay.
the main concern is before SSO fiori link is opening fine after SSO its redirect to SSO page of netweaver which takes time some.
 
Tested the URL on multiple devices (Android, iPhone, Windows laptops).
Observed the behavior across different networks (organization's Wi-Fi and 4G) both networks sometimes opened and some times not.

in the short "url sometimes open without any delay in andriod phone and laptop somestimes not"

entry.title: Previewing Fiori Standard Extension Project on BAS
entry.content:
Do anyone know how can I preview an Extension Project on SAP Business Application Studio ?
I manage to preview other custom Fiori Apps, but cant preview any of the Extension Projects of Standard Fiori Projects, such as Look Up Retail Products.
Is there a version prerequisite or any steps that was missing ?

Many Thanks.

entry.title: SAPUI5 Treetable client-side Binding without any ODATA SERVICE/Backend by using mockserver
entry.content:
Happy new year fellas!✨🎉
So I was playing around with the Treetable in SAPUI5, I had no backend-API/ODataservice to get the data from to bind my table with. Then i decided to go with the MockServer(Middleware)

 


We will navigate through the following steps :

STEP 1 : Creating a project in SAP BAS/VSCode IDE using metadata document - 

**NOTE - If you are using VSCode, you can directly enter the path of the metadata.xml ,and if you are using SAP BAS, you have to upload the metadata.xml file to your projects folder before going for project creation.(I did the same😅) ,sharing my metadata.xml if you in case you wanna use mine.

 

 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" standalone="yes"?>
<edmx:Edmx Version="1.0"
xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2008/09/edm"
xmlns:edmx="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/06/edmx"
xmlns:m="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/metadata"
xmlns:edmanno="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2009/02/edm/annotation"
xmlns:sap="http://www.sap.com/Protocols/SAPData">

<edmx:DataServices m:DataServiceVersion="2.0">
    <Schema Namespace="DemoModel" xml:lang="en" sap:schema-version="1">
        
        <EntityType Name="Node">
            <Key>
                <PropertyRef Name="NodeID" />
            </Key>
            <Property Name="NodeID" Type="Edm.Int32" Nullable="false" edmanno:StoreGeneratedPattern="Identity" sap:hierarchy-node-for="NodeID"/>
            <Property Name="HierarchyLevel" Type="Edm.Int32" Nullable="false" sap:hierarchy-level-for="NodeID"/>
            <Property Name="Description" Type="Edm.String" Nullable="false" MaxLength="40" Unicode="true" FixedLength="false" />
            <Property Name="ParentNodeID" Type="Edm.Int32" Nullable="true" sap:hierarchy-parent-node-for="NodeID"/>
            <Property Name="DrillState" Type="Edm.String" Nullable="false" MaxLength="10" Unicode="true" FixedLength="false" sap:hierarchy-drill-state-for="NodeID" />
        </EntityType>
        
        <EntityContainer Name="DemoEntities" edmanno:LazyLoadingEnabled="true" m:IsDefaultEntityContainer="true">
            <EntitySet Name="Nodes" EntityType="DemoModel.Node" />
        </EntityContainer>
        
    </Schema>
</edmx:DataServices>
</edmx:Edmx>

 

 

STEP 2: Check the dependency that the mock server is installed.

And Also in ui5-mock.yaml if your mockdataPath is there is not.

**NOTE - If you need to install mock server manually to a project, see the following.

Install MockServer From Here 

STEP 3: Create your json file having your mock data in "webapp/localService/data/your_filename.json". my json(Nodes.json) is given below.

 

[
    {
        "NodeID": 1,
        "HierarchyLevel": 0,
        "Description": "Company",
        "ParentNodeID": null,
        "DrillState": "expanded"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 2,
        "HierarchyLevel": 0,
        "Description": "Departments",
        "ParentNodeID": null,
        "DrillState": "expanded"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 3,
        "HierarchyLevel": 0,
        "Description": "Projects",
        "ParentNodeID": null,
        "DrillState": "expanded"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 4,
        "HierarchyLevel": 1,
        "Description": "HR",
        "ParentNodeID": 1,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 5,
        "HierarchyLevel": 1,
        "Description": "Finance",
        "ParentNodeID": 1,
        "DrillState": "expanded"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 6,
        "HierarchyLevel": 2,
        "Description": "Payroll",
        "ParentNodeID": 5,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 7,
        "HierarchyLevel": 2,
        "Description": "Budgeting",
        "ParentNodeID": 5,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 8,
        "HierarchyLevel": 1,
        "Description": "IT",
        "ParentNodeID": 2,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 9,
        "HierarchyLevel": 1,
        "Description": "Marketing",
        "ParentNodeID": 2,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 10,
        "HierarchyLevel": 1,
        "Description": "Sales",
        "ParentNodeID": 2,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 11,
        "HierarchyLevel": 1,
        "Description": "Project A",
        "ParentNodeID": 3,
        "DrillState": "expanded"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 12,
        "HierarchyLevel": 2,
        "Description": "Phase 1",
        "ParentNodeID": 11,
        "DrillState": "expanded"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 13,
        "HierarchyLevel": 3,
        "Description": "Task 1",
        "ParentNodeID": 12,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 14,
        "HierarchyLevel": 3,
        "Description": "Task 2",
        "ParentNodeID": 12,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 15,
        "HierarchyLevel": 3,
        "Description": "Task 3",
        "ParentNodeID": 12,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 16,
        "HierarchyLevel": 3,
        "Description": "Task 4",
        "ParentNodeID": 12,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    }
]

 

STEP 4 : XML View with TreeTable Control. (If you are using Fiori Elements , the process is same upto here, and you should get the preview with hierachical data in table after step 3, just make sure your metadata is having the hierarchy oData annotations like my metadata, else it wont work😓).

 

<mvc:View controllerName="ztreetable.controller.View1"
    xmlns="sap.ui.table"
    xmlns:m="sap.m"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc">
    <m:Page id="page" >
        <TreeTable
            id="treeTable"
            selectionMode="Single"
            enableColumnReordering="false"
            rows="{
                path : '/Nodes',
                parameters : {
                    countMode: 'Inline',
                    numberOfExpandedLevels: 1
                }
            }">

            <columns>

                <Column>
                    <m:Label text="Description"/>
                    <template>
                        <m:Link text="{Description}" wrapping="false" />
                    </template>
                </Column>

                <Column>
                    <m:Label text="HierarchyLevel"/>
                    <template>
                        <m:Text text="{HierarchyLevel}" wrapping="false" />
                    </template>
                </Column>

                <Column>
                    <m:Label text="NodeID"/>
                    <template>
                        <m:Text text="{NodeID}" wrapping="false" />
                    </template>
                </Column>

                <Column>
                    <m:Label text="ParentNodeID"/>
                    <template>
                        <m:Text text="{ParentNodeID}" wrapping="false" />
                    </template>
                </Column>

            </columns>

        </TreeTable>
    </m:Page>
</mvc:View>

 

STEP 5 : Preview your app using npm run start-mock 😎 (DONT USE NORMAL PREVIEW SCRIPT).
**NOTE - Make sure all your paths are correct and specially the name of the json file should be same as the pathname of "rows" attribute of TreeTable Control.

entry.title: Fiori Elements Developer Extensibility Doubt
entry.content:
Hello Community
I am trying to adapt a screen of the ES5 demo Gateway through 'SAP Business Studio'. My question is how can I open the 'SAP UI5 Visual Editor' to add new fields to the screen?When I right-click on the 'manifest', the 'Open SAP UI5 Visual Editor' option is not displayed (see attachment 1). I tried the 'Open Adaptation Editor' option but nothing happens.

entry.title: Issue with hasdraftentity in sap cap odata v4
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I have worked on list report based on SAP CAP, when i select any line item i get below error, Any suggestions are appreciated

Error : Failed to drill-down into (ID=48243eff-54a4-4e0d-b7b6-5f11057bc663,IsActiveEntity=true)/HasDraftEntity, invalid segment: HasDraftEntity - monitor/Invoices?$count=true

Error : Failed to drill-down into (ID=48243eff-54a4-4e0d-b7b6-5f11057bc663,IsActiveEntity=true)/DraftAdministrativeData, invalid segment: DraftAdministrativeData - monitor/Invoices?$count=true&$orderby=IssueDate%20desc

entry.title: Deployment Error : 403 error in Fiori App deployed in S4Hana Public cloud
entry.content:
Hi SAP-ians,

I am currently working on an integration project between SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and a External API. 
I am facing an issue with my Fiori app deployed on the S/4HANA Public Cloud. The app is working fine locally, but when deployed on the cloud, I am encountering a 403 Forbidden error when attempting to access backend data.

The solution involves the following steps:

  1. CDS and CAP Application:

    • I have created a Core Data Services (CDS) view in public cloud and consumed it in SAP BTP Business Application Studio (BAS).
    • I have developed a CAP application where I consumed the CDS OData service and implemented the backend logic for posting data to the External API.

  2. Deployment to SAP BTP:

    • The CAP backend is deployed to Cloud Foundry in SAP BTP, and I have created a destination for the deployed BAS service URL, which I am using to connect the Fiori app to the backend.
    • I have also tested my CAP backend service in Postman it is working fine.

       


      In Cap Destination I have also tried using the Authentication type as OAuth2clientCredentials.

  3. Fiori App Development:

    • I created a Fiori app that consumes the backend data from the CAP application. (the Fiori and CAPM are two different Projects in BAS).
    • The app works fine when running locally in the SAP BTP BAS environment. I'm able to interact with the external API through CAP backend as expected.

       

  4. Deployment to S/4HANA Public Cloud:

    • I successfully deployed the Fiori app as a Fiori tile to the S/4HANA Public Cloud.
    • In public Cloud, While accessing the deployed Application, the UI of the app loads correctly, I am facing a 403 error when trying to access the backend data (while loading the metadata of the backend).

       

  5. IAM and Authorization:

    • While Deployment, I have created the IAM App in ADT for the app, but since I am using a CAP backend (not RAP), I am unsure of how to bind the service of CAP to the Application to resolve the 403 error. Typically, the RAP service is bound to the app to resolve such issues, but since I am not using RAP, I need guidance on how to handle this scenario. I didn't added any services to this APP.

       

    • Additionally, I see a UAA roles is created in BTP environment up on deployment of CAPM application, if I assign the role to user will it resolve this 403 error.

       

My Questions:

  • What could be causing the 403 error when accessing the CAP backend from the deployed Fiori app on S/4HANA Public Cloud? is it due to lack of authorization or roles in BTP?
  • Are there specific configurations or steps I need to follow when using a CAP backend instead of RAP for Fiori apps deployed on S/4HANA Public Cloud?
  • Is there any option available to import the CAP service to ABAP environment so that I could add the CAP service to application in IAM?
  • How can I resolve the 403 error and ensure that the Fiori app can access the backend data?
  • Will the error resolve if I assign the UAA roles to the respective User in BTP?

Any insights or guidance would be greatly appreciated!

Many Thanks in Advance!


Best Regards,
Arasu

SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA ,SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: MTB build failure after upgraded to npm 11
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We had a MTA project was deployed on BTP cloud foundry,

Initially, we are using node v18, npm 10 without issue.

From current release, BTP required our project to use NPM v11, so that we upgraded all related node modules. And project running on local service without issue.

But when we tried to run the 'Mbt build' to build the mta project (mbt version: v1.2.34),  we are facing following error,

and we checked the "boast" files, both of them are found in respective location.

[2025-01-05 18:02:52]  INFO the build results of the "raasapp2-srv" module will be packaged and saved in the "/Users/i3****/Documents/*****/working/raasdata_project/raasdashboard/raasdatatst4/.raasdatatst4_mta_build_tmp/raasapp2-srv" folder
[2025-01-05 18:03:01] ERROR could not package the "raasapp2-srv" module when archiving: could not read the "/Users/i3****/Documents/****/working/raasdata_project/raasdashboard/raasdatatst4/node_modules/.bin/boast" symbolic link: stat /Users/i3****/Documents/****/working/raasdata_project/raasdashboard/raasdatatst4/node_modules/swagger2openapi/boast.js: no such file or directory
make: *** [raasapp2-srv] Error 1
[2025-01-05 18:03:01] ERROR could not build the MTA project: could not execute the "make -f Makefile_20250105180232.mta p=cf mtar= strict=true mode=" command: exit status 2
Error: could not build the MTA project: could not execute the "make -f Makefile_20250105180232.mta p=cf mtar= strict=true mode=" command: exit status 2

 following are the dependencies in package.json

"engines": {
    "node": "^20",
    "npm": "11.0.0"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "4": "^0.0.0",
    "6": "0.0.1",
    "@cap-js-community/odata-v2-adapter": "^1.13.8",
    "@sap_oss/alert-notification-client": "^1.7.0",
    "@sap-cloud-sdk/http-client": "^3.25.0",
    "@sap-cloud-sdk/mail-client": "^3.18.1",
    "@sap/audit-logging": "^5.8.1",
    "@sap/cds": "^8.6.0",
    "@sap/cds-compiler": "^5.6.0",
    "@sap/cds-dk": "^8.6.1",
    "@sap/hdbext": "^8.1.0",
    "@sap/logging": "^6",
    "@sap/textbundle": "^4.3.0",
    "@sap/xsenv": "^5.4.0",
    "@sap/xssec": "^4.2.7",
    "axios": "^1.7.9",
    "cap-llm-plugin": "^1.4.5",
    "cds-swagger-ui-express": "^0.10.0",
    "cheerio": "^1.0.0-rc.12",
    "cors": "^2.8.5",
    "csv-parse": "^5.5.5",
    "express": "^4",
    "fast-csv": "^4.3.6",
    "generic-pool": "^3.9.0",
    "hana-cli": "^3.202405.1",
    "hdb": "^0.19.8",
    "passport": "^0.6.0",
    "pdfkit": "^0.13.0",
    "pdfkit-table": "^0.1.99",
    "request": "^2.88.2",
    "sap-hdbext-promisfied": "^2.202410.1",
    "uuid": "^11.0.3"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@cap-js/cds-typer": "^0.31.0",
    "@sap/eslint-plugin-ui5-jsdocs": "^2.0.8",
    "@sap/ux-specification": "^1.124.7",
    "@sapui5/ts-types": "^1.92.2",
    "chai": "^4.3.10",
    "chai-as-promised": "^7.1.1",
    "chai-subset": "^1.6.0",
    "eslint": "^9.17.0",
    "jest": "^29.7.0"
  },

following is the mta.yaml

_schema-version: "3.1"
ID: raasapp2
description: RaaS CAP project.
version: 1.0.22
modules:
- name: raasapp2-srv
  type: nodejs
  path: .
  requires:
  - name: raasapp2-db
  - name: uaa_raasapp2
  - name: raas_email_srv
  provides:
  - name: srv-api
    properties:
      srv-url: ${default-url}
  parameters:
    buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
    disk-quota: 2048M
    memory: 2048M
    stack: cflinuxfs4
  build-parameters:
    #builder: npm-ci
    ignore:
    - '*default-env.json*'
    - ./db/*.env*
    #- ./db/node_modules
    - ./app
    #- ./app/node_modules
    - ./mta_archives
    - ./db/ddl
    - ./db/dml
    - .git
    - .*.DS_Store
    - .*/*/.DS_Store
    - .*/*/*/.DS_Store


- name: raasapp2-db-deployer
  type: hdb
  path: db
  requires:
  - name: raasapp2-db
    properties:
      TARGET_CONTAINER: ~{hdi-service-name}
  # - name: cross-container-service-1
  #   group: SERVICE_REPLACEMENTS
  #   properties:
  #     key: ServiceName_1
  #     service: ~{the-service-name}
  parameters:
    buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
    stack: cflinuxfs4
  build-parameters:
    ignore:
    - default-env.json
    - .env
    #- '*node_modules*'


- name: app
  type: approuter.nodejs
  path: app
  requires:
  - name: uaa_raasapp2
  - name: srv-api
    group: destinations
    properties:
      forwardAuthToken: true
      name: srv-api
      url: ~{srv-url}
  parameters:
    disk-quota: 256M
    memory: 256M
    keep-existing-routes: true
    stack: cflinuxfs4
  build-parameters:
    ignore:
    - default-env.json
    - .env
    #- '*node_modules*'

resources:
- name: raasapp2-db
  type: com.sap.xs.hdi-container
  parameters:
    service: hana
    service-plan: hdi-shared
  properties:
    hdi-service-name: ${service-name}
# - name: cross-container-service-1
#   type: org.cloudfoundry.existing-service
#   parameters:
#     service-name: CC_ACCESS
#   properties:
#     the-service-name: ${service-name}
- name: uaa_raasapp2
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    path: ./xs-security.json
    service: xsuaa
    service-name: raasapp2-xsuaa-service
    service-plan: application
- name: raas_email_srv
  type: org.cloudfoundry.existing-service
  parameters:
    service-name: raas_email_service
  properties:
    the-service-name: ${service-name}
parameters:
  enable-parallel-deployments: true
build-parameters:
  before-all:
  - builder: custom
    commands:
    #- npm config set registry https://registry.npmjs.com/
    #- npm ci
    - npx -p @SAP/cds-dk@latest cds build --production

can anyone give some clue to fix the problem?
